conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a response email to your employee, approving their requested vacation days.
org_constraint: The email must be tailored for a professional audience, ensuring clarity and avoiding jargon.
new_constraint: Include the phrase "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!" in the email.
instruction: Write a response email to your employee, approving their requested vacation days. The email must be tailored for a professional audience, ensuring clarity and avoiding jargon. Include the phrase "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!" in the email.
response:
Subject: Vacation Request Approved - [Employee Name]

Dear [Employee Name],

This email confirms the approval of your requested vacation time from [Start Date] to [End Date].

Please ensure all your urgent tasks are completed or properly handed off to [Colleague's Name, if applicable] before your departure to ensure a smooth workflow while you are out.

No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!

We look forward to having you back in the office on [Return Date].

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Title]<turn|>
judged_response:
Subject: Vacation Request Approved - [Employee Name]

Dear [Employee Name],

This email confirms the approval of your requested vacation time from [Start Date] to [End Date].

Please ensure all your urgent tasks are completed or properly handed off to [Colleague's Name, if applicable] before your departure to ensure a smooth workflow while you are out.

No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!

We look forward to having you back in the office on [Return Date].

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Title]
evaluation_result:
**Step 1**: The extracted content from the Model Response.  
- "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!"  
- The email includes informal language such as "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!" which is not tailored for a professional audience.

**Step 2**:  
- Instruction 1 requires the inclusion of the phrase "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!" This phrase is present in the Model Response, so this constraint is fully satisfied.  
- Instruction 2 requires the email to be tailored for a professional audience, avoiding jargon. The inclusion of informal language such as "No worries, you’re all set for your vacay!" makes the email less professional, so this constraint is not fully satisfied.

**Step 3**: 1
